sanskrit: सर्वाणीन्द्रियकर्माणि प्राणकर्माणि चापरे। आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ जुह्वति ज्ञानदीपिते।।
transliteration: sarvāṇīndriya-karmāṇi prāṇa-karmāṇi cāpare | ātma-saṃyama-yogāgnau juhvati jñāna-dīpite
literal_meaning: All sense-functions and life-breath functions others offer into the self-restraint-yoga-fire, kindled by the urge for knowledge.
contextual_meaning: A yogi redirects every impulse—sensory, motor, respiratory—into the inner fire of disciplined awareness, converting raw energy into self-knowledge.
psychological_state: Flow-state achieved through deliberate redirection of attention from external stimuli to internal regulatory processes.
trigger_scenario: A professional turns off notifications, sits, and consciously slows each breath while observing the urge to check the phone, using the urge itself as fuel for concentration.
anchor_text: Feed every sense-breath impulse into the steady flame of mindful knowing.
